steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The text before the blank—”Leibniz wheel calculators were popular in the first half of the twentieth”—is an independent clause. The text after the blank—”these ingenious devices were eventually replaced by electronic calculators”—is also an independent clause. You're connecting two independent clauses.

steps.title: Apply the comma-and-conjunction rule
steps.content: A comma with a coordinating conjunction joins two independent clauses. The word “but” captures the contrast between the calculators being popular in the early twentieth century and their eventual replacement. Choices A and B fail to properly join independent clauses (creating comma splice and fused sentence, respectively), while D creates ungrammatical syntax. That matches choice C — C is correct.
